brute_force_culling_revived.detail.cull_: Removes entities that are not visible to the player in order to reduce rendering load.
Removes block entities that are hidden from view to improve performance.
Skips rendering chunks that are completely invisible to the player.
brute_force_culling_revived.detail.async: Uses a separate thread to rebuild visible chunks asynchronously and then synchronizes them with the main rendering thread. This can improve frame rates during movement by speeding up chunk generation. Synchronization may cause short delays when rotating the camera quickly, which can make edge chunks load more slowly. You can increase the number of chunks processed in parallel in the game settings to help. This feature is experimental. If crashes occur while chunks are loading, disable this option.
brute_force_culling_revived.detail.sampl: Controls the scaling ratio of the depth texture used for culling. A higher ratio produces more accurate culling and better visual results. If performance is not affected, you can raise the ratio as much as possible. Avoid lowering it below 20 percent.
brute_force_culling_revived.detail.culli: Sets how many frames are delayed before the culling texture is updated on the CPU. This prevents incomplete data transmission from causing stuttering in the main thread. Higher values slow down culling speed. You can monitor update times for debugging. When shaders are enabled, data transmission slows down and the delay automatically increases by one frame.
brute_force_culling_revived.detail.check: Testing option that allows you to check depth and culling texture functionality when the monitoring panel is open.
brute_force_culling_revived.detail.debug: Testing option that works only in vanilla Minecraft. It shows which chunks are being culled.
brute_force_culling_revived.detail.nvidi: Warning: Not compatible with Nvidium’s asynchronous BFS feature.
brute_force_culling_revived.detail.auto_: On some devices, using shaders together with asynchronous chunk building can cause crashes. This setting automatically disables asynchronous chunk building when shaders are active.
brute_force_culling_revived.detail.sodiu: Warning: Requires Sodium or Embeddium to function.
brute_force_culling_revived.skip_culling: Block entities are excluded from culling
